algeria: Under Vladimir Petkovic, Algeria is looking to rebuild their golden generation and surprise opponents in the knockout stages with a disciplined, high-pressing setup.
argentina: Lionel Scaloni's reigning world champions enter the tournament with the highest expectations, aiming to defend their crown in Lionel Messi's final international chapter.
australia: Under Tony Popovic, the Socceroos aim to build on their past success by playing a gritty, compact style and qualifying from their group.
austria: Ralf Rangnick has turned Austria into a tactical powerhouse; their high-intensity counter-pressing makes them a dark horse expected to reach the quarter-finals.
belgium: Rudi Garcia takes charge of a transitioning Belgian side, looking to blend remaining veteran leadership with emerging youth to push deep into the knockout rounds.
bosniaandherzegovina: Led by Sergej Barbarez, Bosnia enters with a focus on defensive organization, aiming to fight for a historic round-of-16 berth.
brazil: Under Carlo Ancelotti's legendary management, the Seleção are expected to play fluid, creative football as they chase a historic sixth star.
canada: Under Jesse Marsch's high-tempo style, Canada aims to leverage home-field support and a talented young squad to make a deep knockout run.
capeverde: Led by Bubista, Cape Verde is a rising African power aiming to play brave, attacking football and advance past the group stage.
colombia: Nestor Lorenzo's team is in spectacular form, combining defensive solidity with creative flair to eye a potential semi-final appearance.
croatia: Under Zlatko Dalic, the aging but elite Croatian squad relies on world-class midfield control to engineer another heroic deep run.
curacao: Dick Advocaat's veteran coaching brings defensive structure and belief, aiming to pull off major upsets and qualify for the knockouts.
czechia: Led by Miroslav Koubek, the Czechs combine physical strength and quick transition play to target a spot in the Round of 16.
drcongo: Sebastien Desabre's side is compact and deadly on the counter, aiming to surprise heavyweights and advance deep.
ecuador: Sebastian Beccacece brings a high-intensity, aggressive pressing system to a young, athletic squad eyeing a quarter-final breakthrough.
egypt: Led by legendary Hossam Hassan, Egypt relies on defensive discipline and clinical counter-attacks to progress past the group stage.
england: Under Thomas Tuchel's elite tactical guidance, the Three Lions are under immense pressure to finally bring football home with their world-class squad.
france: Didier Deschamps' powerhouse squad is a top favorite, blending unmatched squad depth and tournament experience to reach the final.
germany: Julian Nagelsmann has modernized Germany's style, expecting nothing less than a dominant run to the championship on home-continent soil.
ghana: Carlos Queiroz brings veteran defensive structure, aiming to guide the Black Stars' explosive young talent into the knockout rounds.
haiti: Sebastien Migne's side brings speed and resilience, looking to embrace the underdog tag and secure historic group stage points.
iran: Under Amir Ghalenoei, Iran relies on a solid defensive block and clinical finishing to target their first-ever progression to the knockout phase.
iraq: Graham Arnold brings a disciplined, physical approach to Iraq, aiming to secure group qualification and make a statement in the Round of 32.
ivorycoast: Emerse Fae's reigning African champions look to carry their momentum into the global stage, targeting a deep run with an athletic, star-studded roster.
japan: Hajime Moriyasu's highly technical squad is poised to showcase fluid transition play, aiming for a historic quarter-final appearance.
jordan: Under Jamal Sellami, Jordan hopes to continue their historic continental rise by playing compact football and challenging group favorites.
mexico: Under Javier Aguirre's passionate leadership, El Tri looks to maximize home support and break their knockout-stage curse.
morocco: Walid Regragui's Atlas Lions aim to replicate their historic 2022 heroics, utilizing a rock-solid defense and rapid transition style.
netherlands: Ronald Koeman's Oranje rely on a world-class defense and tactically flexible setup, expecting to reach at least the quarter-finals.
newzealand: Under Darren Bazeley, the All Whites aim to make their mark on the global stage with a young, ambitious squad fighting for group progression.
norway: Stale Solbakken's side is powered by world-class attacking talent, aiming to secure group qualification and make a deep knockout impact.
panama: Under Thomas Christiansen's possession-based style, Panama seeks to showcase their growth and compete for a historic knockout spot.
paraguay: Gustavo Alfaro brings his signature defensive organization, aiming to make Paraguay extremely difficult to beat and fight for a Round of 32 place.
portugal: Roberto Martinez's squad boasts elite talent in every position, expected to play expansive football and contest for the championship.
qatar: Julen Lopetegui brings European pedigree and tactical refinement, hoping to guide the Asian champions into a surprise knockout run.
saudiarabia: Under Giorgios Donis, Saudi Arabia aims to leverage technical fluidity and disciplined shape to replicate historic group-stage upsets.
scotland: Steve Clarke's gritty, passionate side relies on defensive unity and set-piece strength, aiming to reach the knockouts for the first time.
senegal: Under Pape Thiaw, Senegal combines physical power with tactical versatility, expecting to progress deep into the knockout phase.
southafrica: Hugo Broos' squad brings a cohesive, domestic-based core, aiming to surprise opponents with their chemistry and reach the Round of 32.
southkorea: Under Hong Myungbo's leadership, the Taegeuk Warriors rely on high work rate and elite individual quality to target a knockout berth.
spain: Luis de la Fuente's young, dynamic European champions play possession-based football with direct winger threats, aiming to lift the trophy.
sweden: Graham Potter brings his signature tactical flexibility and fluid possession style, hoping to guide Sweden deep into the knockout rounds.
switzerland: Under Murat Yakin, the highly disciplined and experienced Swiss side is expected to navigate the group stage and serve as a dangerous knockout opponent.
tunisia: Sabri Lamouchi looks to instill defensive resilience and tactical maturity, targeting a competitive group stage showing and a Round of 32 spot.
turkiye: Vincenzo Montella's exciting, youth-filled Turkish side plays dynamic, attacking football, aiming to replicate past deep tournament runs.
uruguay: Marcelo Bielsa's high-pressing, relentless physical style makes Uruguay one of the most feared teams, expecting to contest the latter stages.
usa: Under Mauricio Pochettino's elite guidance, the co-hosts are expected to play aggressive, proactive football to capture the nation's imagination and reach the quarter-finals.
uzbekistan: Fabio Cannavaro's tactical leadership brings defensive focus and star-power belief, aiming to lead the Central Asian debutants into the knockouts
